seoTitle: How to Reset or Change Your n8n Password
metaDescription: Learn how to change your n8n account password from the dashboard, or reset it via terminal CLI commands for self-hosted instances.
intro: Whether you just want to update your password or you're locked out of a self-hosted instance, n8n gives you a couple of paths to regain access. This guide covers changing your password directly from your account settings, plus the terminal-based option available for self-hosted setups.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Go to your n8n dashboard and navigate to your personal profile settings.

steps.heading: Find the password section
steps.body: Look for the security or credentials area where password changes are managed.

steps.heading: Enter your current and new password
steps.body: Verify your current password before entering and confirming your new one.

steps.heading: Save your update
steps.body: Confirm the change so your new password takes effect immediately.

steps.heading: Use CLI recovery if locked out
steps.body: For self-hosted instances, use n8n's terminal commands to reset a forgotten owner password.

faq.question: How do I change my n8n password normally?
faq.answer: You can update it from your personal settings panel by entering your current and new password.

faq.question: What if I'm locked out of a self-hosted n8n instance?
faq.answer: Self-hosted n8n supports terminal CLI commands to reset the owner password when the dashboard is inaccessible.

faq.question: Do I need terminal access to reset my password on n8n Cloud?
faq.answer: Cloud accounts typically manage password resets through the dashboard rather than terminal commands.

faq.question: Will resetting my password affect my existing workflows?
faq.answer: No, a password change doesn't affect your saved workflows or data.
